flags.concern: Payments caps: Recurring debt: the revised amount_token fix still keeps derived USD out of the stored payment row, so admin-chat token-denominated payouts do not reliably participate in the rolling daily USD cap or the success-DM threshold.
flags.evidence: Fixed the single root cause behind all 8 open flags: derived USD was being kept local to the cap check instead of persisted into the stored payment row. Step 8 now explicitly stamps `record['amount_usd']` and `record['token_price_usd']` (and the mirrored request_payload fields) when USD is derived from `amount_token * token_price_usd` for a capped provider — and only in that branch, so uncapped providers and is_test paths keep their existing `amount_usd=None` behavior. This makes the rolling-24h helper (Step 7, which sums stored amount_usd) and the success-DM gate (Step 9, which reads payment.get('amount_usd')) both see the real value for admin-chat amount_token-only payouts end-to-end. Verified safety of existing tests: `test_request_payment_amount_token` at tests/test_admin_payments.py:684-709 uses uncapped provider "solana" and asserts `provider.price_calls == 0`, so our derivation branch (gated on capped_providers) never fires and the test stays green. Same for test_social_publish_service.py:496, which uses uncapped "solana_native". Added three new tests: `test_request_payment_amount_token_path_stamps_amount_usd` (pins the persisted derivation), `test_request_payment_amount_token_uncapped_provider_preserves_none` (regression pin for the existing behavior on uncapped providers), `test_request_payment_rolling_daily_cap_sees_derived_usd` (end-to-end wiring through the rolling helper), and `test_admin_success_dm_sees_derived_usd` (end-to-end wiring through the success-DM gate). Updated assumptions to state that derived USD is persisted when the provider is capped. Updated the end-to-end cap-enforcement success criterion to explicitly require persistence so the `must` matches what the plan actually proves. Added a new invariant forbidding amount_usd stamping for uncapped/is_test paths. No other structural changes; prior fixes to exception-based upsert_wallet blocking, index-aware collision branching, control-flow ordering, and PaymentCog construction-site handling remain unchanged.

flags.concern: Wallet registry updates: The proposed `upsert_wallet` blocked-path sentinel is only handled in admin chat, but `grants_cog` is another live caller and would treat a truthy sentinel dict as a successful wallet record.
flags.evidence: Resolved all 12 open significant flags. (1) Cap enforcement now covers amount_token-only callers (admin_chat final payout): derives USD from provider.get_token_price_usd() and fail-closes to manual_hold if price is unavailable. Added two new tests pinning that path. (2) upsert_wallet blocking is now exception-based (WalletUpdateBlockedError) and both live callers — admin_chat._handle_wallet_received AND grants_cog._start_payment_flow — catch it explicitly, eliminating the truthy-sentinel trap. (3) Collision handling is now index-aware with two explicit branches: Branch A inserts a fresh manual_hold row only when every prior row is failed/cancelled (index permits); Branch B returns None without attempting an insert when a prior row still holds a unique-index slot, and the admin DM fires in both cases. Added a new Branch-B test. (4) Step 5's control flow is fixed: the existing-wallet lookup now runs before the guard, and the sample code matches the real call order. (5) PaymentCog success-DM config is now read inside __init__ from env so both construction sites (main.py:233 and the setup() hook at payment_cog.py:503) stay in sync without touching either caller. (6) Test glue expansion is explicit: inline fakes in test_admin_payments.py and test_social_publish_service.py are named as the sites where has_active_payment_or_intent, get_rolling_24h_payout_usd, and WalletUpdateBlockedError stubs must land. (7) Criteria quality: demoted git-history-audit and log-audit from must to should (they are memo tasks, not diff-verifiable); added must criteria for the two new token-path cap tests and the Branch-B collision test. No scope creep; the plan remains aligned with the original P0/P1/P2 brief.

flags.concern: Payment idempotency: The collision path over-assumes it can insert a new `manual_hold` row for any prior-wallet mismatch, but the active unique index still blocks inserts when the prior row is not `failed` or `cancelled`.

flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the revised Step 8 and Step 9 together against the real admin-chat final payout caller at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:352-365`. The plan now derives USD for amount_token-only requests during cap checks, but its own assumptions say that derived USD is kept local and not written back to `record['amount_usd']`; because `get_rolling_24h_payout_usd` is defined to sum stored `payment_requests.amount_usd` and the success-DM gate in Step 9 checks `payment.get('amount_usd')`, the actual admin-chat payouts would still miss the rolling daily cap and success-DM threshold in the live flow.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The collision path in Step 6 assumes it can create a fresh `manual_hold` row whenever any prior `(producer, producer_ref, is_test)` row has a different wallet, but `sql/payments.sql:126-128` still enforces uniqueness for every status except `failed` and `cancelled`. If the differing prior row is `pending_confirmation`, `queued`, `processing`, `submitted`, `confirmed`, or `manual_hold`, the insert cannot succeed, so the plan needs an explicit non-insert branch instead of assuming a new held row can always be created.

flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: The amount_token issue is broader than the immediate cap check. In this codebase, admin-chat final payouts are token-denominated at creation time, but later observability and rolling-budget logic operate on the stored `amount_usd` field. flags.concern: Does the approach match how the codebase solves similar problems?: The proposed `{'__error__': 'active_payment_or_intent'}` return value does not match the surrounding `DatabaseHandler` convention, which is overwhelmingly `Optional[Dict]` or `bool` with `None`/`False` on failure across `src/common/db_handler.py`. That divergence is risky here because current callers already treat any dict as success.

Step 8 derives USD locally in `PaymentService.request_payment`, Step 7's rolling helper sums stored `payment_requests.amount_usd`, and Step 9's success-DM gate reads `payment.get('amount_usd')`; unless the derived USD is persisted or recomputed in the downstream sites too, the feature is still only partially wired across the repository.
flags.evidence: Fixed the single root cause behind all 8 open flags: derived USD was being kept local to the cap check instead of persisted into the stored payment row. Step 8 now explicitly stamps `record['amount_usd']` and `record['token_price_usd']` (and the mirrored request_payload fields) when USD is derived from `amount_token * token_price_usd` for a capped provider — and only in that branch, so uncapped providers and is_test paths keep their existing `amount_usd=None` behavior. This makes the rolling-24h helper (Step 7, which sums stored amount_usd) and the success-DM gate (Step 9, which reads payment.get('amount_usd')) both see the real value for admin-chat amount_token-only payouts end-to-end. Verified safety of existing tests: `test_request_payment_amount_token` at tests/test_admin_payments.py:684-709 uses uncapped provider "solana" and asserts `provider.price_calls == 0`, so our derivation branch (gated on capped_providers) never fires and the test stays green. Same for test_social_publish_service.py:496, which uses uncapped "solana_native". Added three new tests: `test_request_payment_amount_token_path_stamps_amount_usd` (pins the persisted derivation), `test_request_payment_amount_token_uncapped_provider_preserves_none` (regression pin for the existing behavior on uncapped providers), `test_request_payment_rolling_daily_cap_sees_derived_usd` (end-to-end wiring through the rolling helper), and `test_admin_success_dm_sees_derived_usd` (end-to-end wiring through the success-DM gate). Updated assumptions to state that derived USD is persisted when the provider is capped. Updated the end-to-end cap-enforcement success criterion to explicitly require persistence so the `must` matches what the plan actually proves. Added a new invariant forbidding amount_usd stamping for uncapped/is_test paths. No other structural changes; prior fixes to exception-based upsert_wallet blocking, index-aware collision branching, control-flow ordering, and PaymentCog construction-site handling remain unchanged.

flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the actual admin payout caller and the downstream consumer. `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:352-365` still creates final payouts with `amount_token` only, while the planned success-DM gate in `PaymentCog` keys off `amount_usd`; with the revised assumption that derived USD stays local instead of being stored, the real caller shape is still not handled end-to-end.

flags.concern: Is there convincing verification for the change?: There is still no test that an amount_token-only admin-chat payout contributes to the rolling-24h USD total or that a confirmed amount_token-only payout can cross the success-DM threshold. Because the revised plan keeps derived USD out of the stored row, those two real behaviors can still be wrong while every newly listed test passes.

flags.concern: Confirmation flow: null-recipient rejection is not actually wired to a uniform admin-DM path even though a `must` success criterion claims it is.
flags.evidence: Step 1 only tightens `confirm_payment` to log and return `None`. The revised plan does not add an `on_cap_breach`-style callback or any other shared admin-DM mechanism for null-recipient rejections, while the `must` criteria still say that null-recipient paths trigger an admin DM.
